questions.text: When you're setting up a new place—whether it's an Airbnb or a longer-term rental—what's your approach to making it feel like yours? Do you just unpack and live in it as-is, or do you actively decorate with plants, artwork, or personal items? Any specific aesthetic or vibe you try to create?

When you're going through a rough patch emotionally—stress, anxiety, feeling down—what's your first instinct for how you handle it? Do you talk to your partner, prefer alone time to process, reach out to friends, or maybe journal or exercise? And have you ever tried therapy or specific mental health tools that actually helped?

What's your ideal climate feel—like, the weather that makes you feel most alive and energized? Do you need dry heat, embrace the humidity, crave four distinct seasons, or thrive in that tropical year-round warmth? And has your preference changed as you've traveled around?

How do you and your partner keep the relationship feeling fresh and connected over time? Is it through regular date nights, traveling together, having separate hobbies that you then share with each other, little daily rituals, or something else entirely?

How would you describe your sense of humor? Are you the one making jokes, sarcasm flys out of you, you appreciate clever wordplay, or maybe you're more of a dry/absurd humor person? And do you like being around people who make you laugh, or do you prefer your humor to be more low-key?
